title: Mildly Spiced Indian Shrimp Samosas
description: A crowd-pleasing Indian dish featuring shrimp paste and wrapped in pandan leaves, perfect for a snack or appetizer. This recipe is low-glycemic-friendly and made with cultured ingredients.
instructions: Heat oil in a pan over medium heat and saute the onion, ginger, and garlic until softened.
Add the cumin seeds and coriander powder and cook for 1 minute.
Add the shrimp and cook until pink and fully cooked.
Stir in the shrimp paste and cook for 1 minute.
Lay a pandan leaf flat and place a tablespoon of the shrimp mixture in the center.
Fold the leaf into a triangle and press the edges to seal.
Repeat with the remaining leaves and filling.
Serve the samosas hot with a dipping sauce of your choice.
To blanch the namul, briefly submerge the pandan leaves in boiling water and then immediately submerge in an ice bath.
To season the namul, sprinkle with salt and let it sit for 10 minutes before rinsing and using.
